expectations.text: Explains that colab exec -f requires relative paths, not absolute
expectations.evidence: Lines 7-8: 'colab exec -f /content/script.py -- -f means "read this file from the local filesystem and execute its contents on the remote runtime."' and lines 12-14: 'Run exec with the local path to your script, not the remote path: ... colab exec -f script.py'

expectations.text: Shows correct command: colab exec -f script.py (without /content/ prefix)

expectations.evidence: No mention of the working directory being /content/ anywhere in the output

expectations.text: Mentions stdin pipe as alternative: echo 'code' | colab exec
expectations.evidence: No mention of stdin piping or echo 'code' | colab exec anywhere in the output
